variant_axes.notes: shadcn uses CVA with `variant` and `size` as the two standard axes. No `colorScheme` — colors come from CSS variables (`--primary`, `--destructive`, etc.) defined in globals.css.

Button.css_classes_base: inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 whitespace-nowrap rounded-md text-sm font-medium ring-offset-background transition-colors focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2 disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50

Input.css_classes_base: flex h-10 w-full rounded-md border border-input bg-background px-3 py-2 text-sm

token_slots.description: shadcn uses CSS variables in globals.css. Tailwind colors reference them (`bg-primary`, `text-primary-foreground`).
token_slots.notes: CSS variables in shadcn use HSL space-separated format (`0 0% 100%` not `hsl(0, 0%, 100%)`) so Tailwind can apply opacity modifiers. Convert DESIGN.md hex values to this format when mapping.

generation_notes: shadcn components are copy-pasted into the user's repo (`components/ui/`) — there's no import from an npm package. The library mapping here is for shape reference only.
CVA is the variant engine — each component has a `{component}Variants` function exporting `cva(...)`. Read the component source to get the exact variant list.
`variant=link` has no background ever — just underlined text.
Radius comes from the shared `--radius` CSS variable (default 0.5rem). All components reference it.
Focus ring uses the shared `--ring` CSS variable and appears on all interactive components via `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring`.
Dark mode is class-based (`.dark` on <html>) — DESIGN.md tokens for dark mode map to the `.dark` section of globals.css.
